$comment: D-AS-REGALLOC-ARG-REGISTER-OCCUPIED (c75 correctness fix, implicit-clobber sibling). A c75 re-audit caught a SILENT MISCOMPILE of integer division under register pressure: reserve-K reserves the non-arg caller-saved register rax as spill-reload scratch, and the rewriter's operand-reload path did not forbid the CURRENT instruction's implicit registers, so a spilled idiv DIVISOR reloaded into rax (x86 idiv's dividend) and clobbered it -> the division computed 3/3=1 instead of 121/3=40 -> 121 instead of 160. FIX (lir_rewrite.cpp rewriteOneFunc): forbid the instruction's own implicitRegisters (inputOrdinals union clobberedOrdinals, e.g. idiv rax+rdx) as reload scratch for its operands + result — the SAME union collectImplicitClobberPositions builds for the allocator's crossing-range exclusion, applied at the op where the transient reload happens (no idiv special-case; per-opcode schema-driven). RED-ON-DISABLE: revert -> x86_64 returns 121 (WRONG). arm64 has no rax/rdx idiv constraint (correct either way). probe(1..15): q=121/3=40, rest=7+..+15=99, early=1+..+6=21 => 160.
